metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve AC5 into a concrete, binary outcome: either mandate the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid (one file serving both triggers) or explicitly require two separate files — remove 'decide'/'optionally'/'recommend' language so an agent can verify the criterion is met.
findings.finding: AC5 defers a real design choice to the executor by using 'decide' + '(recommend)' without committing to an outcome, making it a placeholder rather than a resolved acceptance criterion. An agent cannot determine whether AC5 is satisfied without knowing whether the hybrid file IS required or merely suggested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'decide test.yml convergence (recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5 with: 'Author the workflow as `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` supporting both `workflow_dispatch` (Gerrit g2p inputs) and `push`/`pull_request` triggers (manual runs), so `test.yml` can be retired; SHA-pin all third-party actions.' This commits to the hybrid and makes the criterion binary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the filename ambiguity: commit to a single deliverable name (`gerrit-verify.yaml` OR `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml`) and ensure it matches exactly what g2p's `workflow` config key will reference.
findings.finding: The plan names TWO different filenames for the primary deliverable: 'What'/'Scope' say `gerrit-verify.yaml`, but AC5 says 'decide test.yml convergence (recommend the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid)' — leaving the executor without a definitive edit target. `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` does not exist and is not confirmed as the artifact to create.
findings.suggested_fix: Pin the deliverable to `gerrit-verify.yaml` (the name used in 'What'/'Scope' and consistent with the ~96 ONAP/O-RAN-SC repos). If the hybrid approach is chosen, rename the primary artifact in 'What'/'Scope' to match and update the g2p config accordingly.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the deliverable filename across all sections: either rename the file to `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` in What/Scope to match AC5's recommendation, or update AC5 to reference `gerrit-verify.yaml` — pick one canonical name and use it everywhere.
findings.finding: The deliverable filename is stated as `gerrit-verify.yaml` in the 'What' and 'Scope' sections, but AC5 recommends the file be named `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` for the hybrid approach — two different filenames for the same deliverable across sections.
findings.suggested_fix: If the hybrid (single file for both Gerrit dispatch and manual runs) is the preferred approach, update the What and Scope sections to read `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` and drop the 'optionally converge' hedge; otherwise, keep `gerrit-verify.yaml` everywhere and remove the AC5 filename recommendation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2/AC3: Specify the dependency chain (`build-and-test needs: [clear-vote]`) and define the fallback behaviour when clear-vote fails — either the workflow must abort (preventing a vote without a prior reset) or AC4 must detect and handle a skipped clear-vote state explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback for the case where the clear-vote job itself fails (e.g., SSH connectivity to Gerrit is down when the workflow starts). If clear-vote fails, the subsequent build-and-test and vote jobs may still run (depending on needs: dependencies), and the vote job's if: always() could cast a vote on a Verified label that was never successfully cleared, potentially voting on a stale patchset state. The plan's fail-closed guarantee assumes clear-vote succeeds or the whole workflow aborts, but this sub-step failure mode and its handling are unspecified.
findings.location: AC2: `clear-vote` job; AC1: overall workflow design

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate which third-party actions from `test.yml` (at minimum `actions/checkout`, `actions/setup-python`) require SHA resolution when copied into `gerrit-verify.yaml`, and decide whether `test.yml` itself should be updated to SHA-pin those same actions as part of this story.
findings.finding: The plan requires SHA-pinning all third-party actions in `gerrit-verify.yaml` (AC5), but the `test.yml` steps it copies into `build-and-test` use version-tag references (`actions/checkout@v5`, `actions/setup-python@v6`) rather than SHA pins. The plan does not enumerate `test.yml` as requiring SHA-pin updates, yet the executor must resolve concrete SHAs for these actions in the new file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `test.yml` reuse mechanism in the ACs: state explicitly whether the plan calls for (a) adding `on: workflow_call` to `test.yml` and calling it via `uses:`, (b) extracting shared steps into a composite action, or (c) accepted copy-paste with a documented sync obligation.
findings.finding: The plan's title promises 'reuse test.yml' but the ACs do not specify the reuse mechanism — whether the build-and-test job copies test.yml steps verbatim, calls it via `workflow_call` (requires test.yml to expose `on: workflow_call`), or uses a composite action. Without a defined mechanism, 'reuse' may devolve into copy-paste duplication, causing the exact drift the plan says it wants to avoid.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the pinning strategy inconsistency: either (a) adopt SHA-pinning repo-wide (updating existing workflows) and document the policy, or (b) explicitly state in the plan why `gerrit-verify.yaml` alone warrants SHA-pinning while the remaining workflows keep semver tags.
findings.finding: The plan mandates SHA-pinning for all third-party actions in `gerrit-verify.yaml`, but every existing workflow in `.github/workflows/` uses semver-tag pinning (`@v5`, `@v4`, `@v6`). Introducing SHA-pinning exclusively in `gerrit-verify.yaml` creates an inconsistent pinning policy across the workflow suite with no stated rationale for the asymmetry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC5 whether 'SHA-pin all third-party actions' applies only to the new lfreleng-actions/* and im-open/* actions or also to actions/checkout and actions/setup-python (which test.yml currently uses with tag pins), so the executor does not have to invent this policy boundary.
findings.finding: The plan states 'SHA-pin all third-party actions' as a requirement for the new workflow, but the existing `.github/workflows/test.yml` uses `actions/checkout@v5` and `actions/setup-python@v6` (tag-pinned, not SHA-pinned). If the new workflow also reuses these same steps verbatim ('reusing the existing steps'), it will inherit tag-pinned references, contradicting the SHA-pin AC for the new file.
findings.location: AC5 — 'SHA-pin all third-party actions'; AC3 — 'then the existing test.yml steps'
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow AC5 to: 'SHA-pin the new lfreleng-actions/* and im-open/* actions; actions/checkout and actions/setup-python may follow the existing tag-pin pattern already used in test.yml.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `needs`/`if` sequencing for `build-and-test` when `clear-vote` fails, and add an observability step (e.g. a Slack/email notification or workflow summary annotation) when the `vote` job itself errors so operators can detect and recover from silent vote-loss.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what happens to `build-and-test` if `clear-vote` fails (Gerrit API unreachable at job start), nor does it specify observability (alert/log) for a failed `vote` job — a failed vote is invisible to operators.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **AC: vote job** — Verify the complete `im-open/workflow-conclusion` output enum against `gerrit-review-action`'s accepted `vote-type` vocabulary, and specify explicit conditional logic (e.g., map `timed_out`/`skipped`/`neutral`/`action_required` → `failure`) rather than relying on an unverified direct pass-through.
findings.finding: The plan wires `vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}` as a direct pass-through from `im-open/workflow-conclusion` to `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`, but the two actions' vocabularies are not verified to align. `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can emit values beyond the plan's acknowledged `success`/`failure`/`cancelled` — including `timed_out`, `skipped`, `neutral`, and `action_required`. The `gerrit-review-action` `vote-type` vocabulary is documented in the epic as `clear|success|failure|cancelled` only. Passing an unrecognized value (e.g., `timed_out`) could silently cast +1, error the step, or leave `Verified=0`, all of which break gate correctness.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bare pass-through with an explicit expression: `vote-type: ${{ (steps.conclusion.outputs.workflow_conclusion == 'success') && 'success' || 'failure' }}` — this collapses all non-success outcomes to `failure` (-1), which is the safe/fail-closed behavior and eliminates the unverified vocabulary dependency entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC4 (`vote` job): Define the full WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION value vocabulary and specify an explicit mapping expression (e.g., `${{ steps.conclusion.outputs.workflow_conclusion == 'success' && 'success' || 'failure' }}`) so that timed_out, skipped, and startup_failure all resolve to a known vote-type value rather than being passed through raw.
findings.finding: The WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION enum produced by im-open/workflow-conclusion is referenced as a direct pass-through to gerrit-review-action vote-type, but the plan does not define the full value vocabulary of WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION nor specify a mapping/filtering rule for values outside the known vote-type set (clear|success|failure|cancelled). im-open/workflow-conclusion can emit values such as 'timed_out', 'skipped', and 'startup_failure' that are not in gerrit-review-action's accepted vote-type vocabulary, and the plan provides no decision rule for handling these — leaving an executor to guess whether to map them to 'failure', pass them through (risking an action error), or add an explicit expression step.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, replace the bare `${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}` pass-through with an explicit ternary: `vote-type: ${{ steps.conclusion.outputs.workflow_conclusion == 'success' && 'success' || 'failure' }}`. This collapses the multi-value WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION enum to the two Gerrit-meaningful outcomes (+1 / -1) and is the pattern used in production ONAP/O-RAN-SC gerrit-verify workflows.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a mapping step in the `vote` job that normalises `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` to the `gerrit-review-action` accepted vocabulary (`success` → `success`; anything else → `failure`) before passing it as `vote-type`, so `timed_out`/`skipped`/`neutral`/`stale`/`action_required` all produce a -1 Verified vote rather than an invalid or missing vote.
findings.finding: `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can output values beyond the `gerrit-review-action` accepted `vote-type` vocabulary. The plan passes `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` directly as `vote-type`, but `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can emit `timed_out`, `skipped`, `neutral`, `stale`, and `action_required` in addition to `success`, `failure`, and `cancelled`. `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` only accepts `clear|success|failure|cancelled`. An unrecognised `vote-type` value (e.g. `timed_out`) will leave `Verified=0` or throw an action error — neither casts the intended -1 vote, creating a fail-open gap for timed-out runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Insert a `run` step before `gerrit-review-action` that maps: `if [[ "$CONCLUSION" == "success" ]]; then echo "vote=success" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT; else echo "vote=failure" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT; fi` and use `vote-type: ${{ steps.map.outputs.vote }}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `permissions: contents: read` (or `permissions: {}`) top-level block to `gerrit-verify.yaml` in AC1, matching the pattern in `test.yml`, so the GITHUB_TOKEN is minimally scoped for this externally-dispatched workflow.
findings.finding: The `gerrit-verify.yaml` workflow has no explicitly declared top-level `permissions:` block in the plan or ACs. Omitting it means the workflow runs with the repository's default token permissions, which are often broader than `contents: read` (e.g., `contents: write`, `pull-requests: write`, `issues: write`). Since this is a boundary-crossing workflow (dispatched from an external Gerrit host), the GITHUB_TOKEN's effective permissions should be explicitly minimized.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to the gerrit-verify.yaml top level:
```yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
```
and call this out explicitly in AC1 of the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the replication-sleep duration a configurable workflow input (with a documented default) and record the derivation basis (or plan a probe/measurement against the actual Gerrit instance to justify the chosen value).
findings.finding: The `sleep` duration for Gerrit replication is asserted as a heuristic with no measurement plan; the correct value is environment-dependent and will vary across deployments.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `GERRIT_REPLICATION_SLEEP_SECONDS` workflow input with a default (e.g. `10`) and use it in the step; include a comment citing the measurement or Gerrit doc that justifies the default.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC: e.g., `actionlint .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` for syntax/structure (ACs 1–3), and a manual `gh workflow run gerrit-verify.yaml` with stubbed GERRIT_* inputs followed by inspection of the workflow conclusion and a Gerrit vote on a test change for AC4.
findings.finding: No proving command or verification step is described for any acceptance criterion — the plan specifies what to author but provides no mechanism to confirm the workflow is syntactically valid, structurally correct, or that the Verified vote is actually cast end-to-end before the plan is considered done.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs) and the plan body

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pre-S6 empirical validation step: dispatch the workflow against a real test change on the Gerrit instance (or a staging mirror) and confirm the `Verified` vote appears correctly at +1 and -1 before activating the gate.
findings.finding: No empirical validation step is planned for the end-to-end vote-casting integration (clear→build→vote) before full adoption as a gate, leaving the correctness of `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` → `Verified` +1/-1 mapping and the `gerrit-review-action` parameter wiring unverified.
findings.location: ## What / AC: `vote` job — `im-open/workflow-conclusion` then `gerrit-review-action vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Record the hybrid-file architectural decision (one file serving both Gerrit dispatch and manual runs, and the rationale for convergence) in an ADR or CLAUDE.md before or alongside the workflow file.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decision — one hybrid file serving both Gerrit dispatch and manual runs (`gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml`) — is recommended but not captured in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update.
findings.location: AC #5 — 'decide `test.yml` convergence (recommend the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid so one file serves Gerrit dispatch + manual runs)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (or a section in CLAUDE.md under 'CI Architecture') documenting: why the hybrid approach was chosen over two separate files, what triggers are supported, and the rule for adding new checks (add to the hybrid file only).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the lifecycle of the `Verified` vote under workflow cancellation (cancel-in-progress): either accept that cancelled runs leave Verified=0 and require a re-trigger, OR add a dedicated cancellation-handler job/step (e.g., a `cancelled()` condition in the vote job) that explicitly casts Verified=-1 or re-casts 0 to signal indeterminate state.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `cancel-in-progress: true` on the concurrency group, but `if: always()` on the `vote` job does NOT execute when a workflow run is externally cancelled in GitHub Actions — only when jobs within the run fail. A mid-flight cancellation (e.g., a re-trigger while `build-and-test` is running) will leave the Gerrit `Verified` vote permanently at 0 until a subsequent run completes, creating a window of misleading gate state with no recovery path defined.
findings.location: AC line 1 (concurrency) + AC line 4 (vote job `if: always()`)
findings.suggested_fix: In the `vote` job condition, use `if: always()` but also handle `cancelled()` explicitly: emit a Verified=-1 (or a distinct message) when `steps.conclusion.outputs.workflow_conclusion == 'cancelled'`, so the vote is never left silently at 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the recommended `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid actually adds trigger surface beyond `test.yml`'s existing `workflow_dispatch: {}`; if not, drop the hybrid and keep `gerrit-verify.yaml` Gerrit-dispatch-only (its nine `GERRIT_*` inputs already make it non-runnable as a plain manual dispatch).
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 recommends a 'gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml' hybrid file 'so one file serves Gerrit dispatch + manual/branch runs', but test.yml already declares `workflow_dispatch: {}` (line 20) and triggers on all branches and PRs. Introducing a second hybrid workflow that also adds manual/branch trigger surface creates config-surface proliferation — contributors must reason about two workflow files with overlapping trigger shapes, and both would run the full test suite on any manually dispatched run.
findings.location: AC5 — 'decide test.yml convergence (recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid so one file serves Gerrit dispatch + manual runs)'
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `gerrit-verify.yaml` triggered solely by `workflow_dispatch` with the nine `GERRIT_*` required inputs (it is already non-runnable without them). Do not add push/PR triggers. test.yml already covers manual and branch CI. Document that `gerrit-verify.yaml` is for g2p dispatch only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` is a real, published action and confirm that `vote-type: clear` (reset to 0), `vote-type: success` (+1), and `vote-type: failure` (-1) are valid input values by reading the action's source or published documentation before coding AC2 and AC4.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` (with `vote-type: clear` in the clear-vote job and `vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}` in the vote job) is the correct action and input interface, but this action does not appear anywhere in the codebase — no workflow file, no docs, no lock file — making both its existence and the `vote-type: clear` / `vote-type: <conclusion>` input contract unverifiable from repo evidence alone.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action` is a real, published action and confirm which input parameter accepts the Gerrit refspec (e.g., `GERRIT_REFSPEC`, `ref`, or similar) before writing AC3.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action` fetches the exact `GERRIT_REFSPEC` for the build-and-test job, but this action is absent from every workflow file and every dependency file in the repository — its existence, input schema, and refspec-fetch semantics are entirely unverified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `im-open/workflow-conclusion`'s published output variable name (confirm it is `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION`) and enumerate all possible values it can emit, then explicitly map every non-success value to `-1` rather than relying on a pass-through to `gerrit-review-action`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `im-open/workflow-conclusion` produces a `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` output that is passed directly as `vote-type` to `gerrit-review-action`, but this action appears nowhere in the codebase, making both the output variable name and the enum values it can produce (and therefore what `gerrit-review-action` must accept) unverifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly require that the `vote` (and `clear-vote`) jobs configure the SSH client to use `StrictHostKeyChecking yes` against the pinned `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` value — confirm `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` wires this correctly, or add an explicit `~/.ssh/known_hosts` setup step before those jobs run.
findings.finding: The plan names `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` as a GitHub repo var but does not require that the SSH client in `gerrit-review-action` enforce `StrictHostKeyChecking yes` against the pinned known-hosts value. Without explicit strict host-key verification, the SSH connection from GitHub Actions runners to Gerrit port 29418 is vulnerable to a MITM that could cause the workflow to cast `Verified +1` to an adversary host — directly subverting the gate's integrity.
findings.location: AC4 — `vote` job / SSH vote path; epic 1fa8 Secrets section (`GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` as repo var)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a setup step before the `clear-vote` and `vote` jobs that writes `$GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` into `~/.ssh/known_hosts` and sets `StrictHostKeyChecking yes` in `~/.ssh/config` for the Gerrit host. Verify (via the action's source) that `gerrit-review-action` passes `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes -o UserKnownHostsFile=~/.ssh/known_hosts` (or equivalent) to the SSH command; document this in the AC as a required configuration.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
